text.title: Deployment, drift detection, and fallback
text.summary: What changes after rollout: monitoring, retraining cadence, and the cascade safety net for when the router clearly mis-routes.

section.title: What to monitor

list.item: Routing distribution: fraction of prompts going to strong vs weak; should be stable week over week.
Quality on a fixed canary set (50-200 labelled prompts) — replay daily, alert if win rate drops >3 pp.
User-visible regressions (thumbs-down rate, retry rate, escalation-to-human rate).
Cost per resolved query — the actual headline metric the router exists to move.

rule.statement: Ship a canary suite of at least 50 labelled prompts and replay it nightly; halt the router and fall back to strong-only if canary win rate drops by 3 percentage points or more for two consecutive days.
rule.rationale: Production preference routers degrade silently under traffic shift. Without a canary the first signal is angry user feedback, weeks late.

section.reference: Compute KL divergence between current-week routing distribution and a reference window; alert when above 0.1.

section.title: Retraining cadence
section.p: Schedule retraining on a fixed cadence; do not wait for drift. Continuously log (prompt, weak_output, strong_output, judge_label) into a labelling queue. Retrain when (a) the queue has 5k new triples or (b) a week has passed, whichever comes first.

rule.statement: Retrain the router on at least the last 4 weeks of preference data plus the original Arena seed; never train only on recent data — recency-only routers forget rare hard prompts.
rule.rationale: RouteLLM authors show that fine-tuning on a sliding 1-week window degrades on rare prompts; mixing in the static Arena seed preserves coverage.

section.title: Cascade as safety net
section.p: Pure routing decides the model BEFORE inference. For high-stakes paths, layer a tiny cascade on top: if the weak-routed answer fails a cheap verifier (regex, schema, judge LLM with score < 0.6), escalate to strong.

example.code: choice = router.route(prompt) # weak | strong ans = choice.client.chat.completions.create(...) if choice == "weak" and verifier.score(prompt, ans) < 0.6: ans = strong_client.chat.completions.create(...)
example.note: Router-first decides cheaply; cascade only fires on a small minority of weak-routed answers, preserving the cost win.

antipattern.description: Always escalating on any verifier signal, regardless of magnitude.
antipattern.reason: Erodes the cost saving the router exists for; verifier noise alone can push 30-50% of weak answers to strong.

section.reference: LMSYS deployment notes — drift, retraining, and cascade fallback in production.
Martian + Accenture enterprise router rollout case study.
